projects.title: Project 1: Moon Phase Observation Diary
projects.description: Read the chapter on 'Keeping Time with the Skies'. Observe the moon starting from 1st May till 31st May. Make a note of the data in your notebook in a table similar to Table 11.1. Record the date, moon phase, and time of observation.

projects.title: Project 2: Planetary System Diagrams
projects.description: Make diagrams to show: 1) Planetary system around the Sun, 2) Phases of the Moon, 3) Solar and Lunar Eclipse positions. Use colored diagrams and proper labeling.

projects.title: Project 3: Working Model - Moon Phases
projects.description: Make a working model to see the phases of the Moon as described in Activity 11.2. Use readily available materials like a ball, flashlight, and dark room to demonstrate the phenomenon.

content.materials: Notebook, drawing sheets, colored pens/pencils, ball (for model), flashlight, dark room
content.submission: Submit your holiday notebook with moon observation table, diagrams, and working model photos/documentation.

grading.belowAverage: Completes moon observation for 15+ days with basic diagrams.
grading.average: Completes full moon observation and diagrams with neat presentation.
grading.bright: Completes all observations, detailed diagrams, and a working model with creative explanation.
